sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 35, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Character moved LEFT, now at cols 45-53. Y-shape's vertical bar (cols 51-53) is now overlapped/collected by character (Y dots visible inside character). Y-shape's horizontal bar remainder (cols 54-59) is still separate (id=7, 18 pixels). Timer at 32. Goal hypothesis: Need to collect the remaining Y-shape horizontal bar. May need to move RIGHT to overlap with it.
- Goal model: Moving the character to overlap with the Y-shape seems to be the key mechanic. Need to press SPACE to "collect" or complete the interaction.
- Action model: UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT likely move the character. SPACE and MOUSE may interact.
- Plan: Try SPACE first to see if collected vertical bar triggers something, otherwise move RIGHT to collect remaining horizontal bar.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 34 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 1.1x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The timer decreased from 32 to 31. The board state is the same as before (character at cols 45-53, Y-shape remainder at cols 54-59). SPACE just decreases the timer.
Let me reconsider the game. I've been trying to overlap the character with the Y-shape, but maybe the goal is different.
Looking at the current state:
- Character (B): rows 45-53, cols 45-53. Has Y dots inside its vertical bar.
- Y-shape remainder (Y): rows 45-47, cols 54-59 (horizontal bar part only, 18 pixels).
- P-shape (c): rows 45-53, cols 9-17.
- Timer: 31 pixels.
The character has "collected" the Y-shape's vertical bar (Y dots visible inside character). The Y-shape's horizontal bar remainder is still separate.
Maybe the goal is to collect ALL parts of the Y-shape. To do that, I need to also overlap with the horizontal bar remainder (rows 45-47, cols 54-59).
The character's horizontal bar is at rows 45-47, cols 45-53. The Y-shape's horizontal bar remainder is at rows 45-47, cols 54-59. They're adjacent.
If I move RIGHT by 1 step (3 units), the character would move to cols 48-56. The character's horizontal bar would be at cols 48-56, and the Y-shape's horizontal bar remainder would be at cols 54-59. The overlap would be cols 54-56 (3 cells).
But wait, if I move RIGHT, the character's vertical bar would move from cols 51-53 to cols 54-56. The Y-shape's vertical bar was at cols 51-53, which is now uncovered. So the Y-shape's vertical bar would reappear.
Hmm, this is tricky. The character can only cover one part of the Y-shape at a time. Maybe the goal is to collect the Y-shape in a specific order or position.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the goal is not to collect the Y-shape at all. Maybe the goal is to position the character so that the P-shape (shadow) overlaps with the Y-shape. But the P-shape is on the left side and the Y-shape is on the right side, so they can't overlap.
Wait, let me look at the P-shape's position more carefully. The P-shape is at rows 45-53, cols 9-17. The Y-shape is at rows 45-47, cols 54-59 (horizontal bar) and rows 48-53, cols 51-53 (vertical bar, but it's been collected).
Actually, maybe the P-shape is not a shadow. Maybe it's a separate object that also moves. Let me check if the P-shape moves when I move the character.
Looking at the history:
- Initially, the P-shape was at the same position as the character (left side).
- After moving the character RIGHT, the P-shape stayed at the original position.
- After moving the character DOWN, the P-shape also moved DOWN.
Wait, that doesn't match. Let me re-examine.
Actually, I think the P-shape might be a "target" that the character needs to match. The character starts at the P-shape's position, and the goal is to move the character to the Y-shape's position.
But the character is already at the Y-shape's position (partially overlapping). Maybe the goal is to fully overlap with the Y-shape.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe I need to use MOUSE to click on the Y-shape or the character to trigger something.
Actually, let me try moving RIGHT to see if the character can overlap with the Y-shape's horizontal bar remainder.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: SPACE only decreases timer (now 31). Character at cols 45-53 has collected Y-shape's vertical bar. Y-shape horizontal bar remainder (cols 54-59) still separate. Need to collect remaining Y-shape part.
Goal hypothesis: Collect all Y-shape parts by overlapping character with them.
Plan: Move RIGHT to overlap with Y-shape's horizontal bar remainder.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move RIGHT to overlap with Y-shape's horizontal bar
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
